etymology.description: 冋 = 冂 (border) + 口 (mouth) -> a space enclosed by borders
etymology.interpretation: The character 冋 (jiōng, 'closed') depicts a mouth 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') within an enclosure represented by 冂 (jiōng, 'border'), symbolizing something that is surrounded or enclosed.

examples.chinese: 这个地方很冋(這個地方很冋)。
他的眼睛半冋(他的眼睛半冋)，看起来很累(看起來很累)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǎnjīng bàn jiōng, kàn qǐlái hěn lèi.
examples.english: His eyes are half-closed, looking very tired.
